zh.Abercorn, Linlithgow, West Lothian, Scot: 英國蘇格蘭韋斯特 洛迪安阿爾可
zh.lifeSummary: Sir William Mure of Abercorn於大約1305年出生在英國蘇格蘭韋斯特 洛迪安阿爾可。他於大約1325年與Mure結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1380年在他的家鄉享年77歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭韋斯特 洛迪安阿爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Mure
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French muer ‘mewer’, i.e. someone who tended to hawks in the cages in which they were kept while moulting. Compare Mew .

variants.name: Mura
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Akio, Hideo, Masao, Nanako, Tsutomu, Yoshi.  French Alphonse, Amie, Henri.
German: altered form of some similar (like-sounding) German or perhaps French surname. It is also common in France (Alsace), where it was first recorded in the form Morrau, later Murra(w).
Italian (southern Sardinia): topographic name or nickname from Sardinian mura ‘mulberry’ or ‘blackberry’.

variants.name: Muir
meanings: Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived on a moor, from Older Scots muir ‘moor’ (an expanse of bare, wild upland country). There has been some confusion with Mure .

variants.name: Morea
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Saverio, Domenic, Ferdinando, Rocco.  Spanish Manuel, Carlos, Cesar, Elvira, Jesus, Mauricio.

Italian (Apulia): from the personal name Morea, originally a habitational name from Morea, the medieval name of the Greek region of Peloponnese, named as the land of mulberry trees, from mōrea ‘mulberry’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from any of the places called Morea, in Badajoz, Navarre, and Lleida provinces.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Sir William Mure of Abercorn was born about 1305, in Abercorn, West Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom. He married Mure about 1325. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died about 1380, in his hometown, at the age of 77, and was buried in Abercorn, West Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Mure of Abercornは約1305年にイギリススコットランドウエスト ロスアンアバーコルンで生まれた。約1325年にMureと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1380年に故郷で77歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドウエスト ロスアンアバーコルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Mure of Abercorn(은)는 대략 1305년 영국 스코틀랜드 웨스트 로스이언 압르코른에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1325년 Mure(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1380년 그의 고향에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 웨스트 로스이언 압르코른에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir William Mure of Abercorn родился около 1305 г. в местности абиркорн, вест лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Mure около 1325 г.
